name: Atlanta, Georgia

rent.notes: Georgia has no residential rental tax. Water, sewage, and trash are separate utility bills (not included in rent).
rent.gigInternetNotes: AT&T Fiber ~58% by FCC data, Google Fiber expanding. Best coverage in Buckhead, Chastain Park, Collier Heights. Gaps in downtown/Midtown. ~75% combined fiber coverage.

electricity.notes: Georgia Power. Heavy A/C (May–Sep), moderate electric heat supplement.

gas.notes: Atlanta Gas Light. Natural gas heating (Dec–Feb), cooking, water heater.

water.notes: City of Atlanta Dept of Watershed Mgmt / DeKalb County Water. Among higher US water rates.

sewage.notes: Billed with water. Atlanta/DeKalb sewer charges.

trash.notes: City of Atlanta Dept of Public Works. Weekly curbside collection included in city services.

utilities.taxes: Georgia exempts residential water/sewer from sales tax. Electricity subject to 4% state + local.
utilities.notes: Electricity (heating+cooling), gas, water, sewage, trash. Internet is in entertainment (Cable+1Gig).

healthcare.notes: Medicare Part B ($174.70/mo) + Medigap Plan G (~$200/mo x2 people) + Part D (~$45/mo x2) + dental/vision plans. Higher premiums reflect macular degeneration specialist coverage and frequent monitoring needs.

petCare.notes: Vet visits, vaccinations, flea/tick year-round, pet insurance

petDaycare.notes: Doggy daycare 2 days/week, large breed rate (~$42/day). Camp Bow Wow, Dogtopia Atlanta. Metro Atlanta pricing slightly higher than rural GA.

petGrooming.notes: Bernese coat maintenance, professional grooming monthly

transportation.notes: Car-dependent but MARTA rail/bus available in core metro

entertainment.notes: Incl cable+1Gig internet, Netflix, Amazon Prime, Apple TV+, dining out (lunch for 2 2x/week w/ tax+tip)

phoneCell.notes: T-Mobile 55+ Essentials. 2 phones, 1.5GB+ high-speed data each, all taxes included.

buffer.notes: Monthly safety buffer for unexpected expenses

medicalOOP.notes: Anti-VEGF injections for macular degeneration (~$300-400 OOP each, 6-10x/yr after Medicare 80%). Specialist visits: ophthalmologist q6-8wks, endocrinologist 2-4x/yr, cardiologist 1-2x/yr. Frequent labs: lipid panel, TSH, A1C, CMP 3-4x/yr each. Both adults: PCP visits quarterly.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

taxes.notes: Estimated monthly income tax on $72,000/yr household income. Based on jurisdiction's effective retiree rate; excludes sales/VAT/property tax.

healthcarePreMedicare.notes: ACA silver benchmark for 2-adult household both age ~60, before subsidies. County-level estimate (Fulton County, GA). Atlanta metro — Ambetter, Anthem, Kaiser compete. Estimated ±15% vs healthcare.gov quote — verify for a specific ZIP before relying on the number.

stateIncomeTax.exemptions: $65K retirement income exclusion per person (65+). SS partially exempt.

acaMarketplace.notes: Atlanta metro — Ambetter, Anthem, Kaiser compete.
acaMarketplace.disclaimer: Estimated ±15% vs healthcare.gov quote — verify for a specific ZIP before relying on the number.

pros: Major metro with world-class healthcare (Emory, CDC)
MARTA rail and bus transit system
Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport — busiest in the world
Diverse food scene and cultural attractions
No state income tax on first $65K retirement income per person
cons: Traffic congestion among worst in the US
Higher cost of living than rural Georgia
Some areas have higher crime rates
